operations.notes: XML surgery to insert <p:bg><p:bgPr><a:blipFill> — python-pptx _Background facade exposes no picture-fill setter. Image part via Image.from_file + ImagePart.new.
Clean replacement at exact captured geometry via slide.shapes.add_picture(). Picture shape present after round-trip.

assembler_patch.inject() works as-is on PptxGenJS-produced OOXML. InjectionRequest.placeholder_name accepts the full marker string directly — no shape-renaming needed. Minor cosmetic: neighbouring body text remains visible behind injected SmartArt.

sequenced_apply.notes: All three ops applied to same file in sequence. Zero interference. PowerPoint Mac opens, saves (forces SmartArt cache regen), exports to PDF without issue.

api_corrections_vs_plan: ImagePart.new signature in plan was (package, blob, ct, ext); real API is (package, Image) — use Image.from_file().
Plan assumed op3 required renaming the SMARTART marker to pptx_native_placeholder_<N>. Real InjectionRequest accepts placeholder_name directly — renaming unnecessary.
Flat-list builder expects data.items as list of strings, not list of dicts. Plan's {'items': [{'text': 'X'}]} failed with FlatListBuildError.
